profile.name: Digvijay Kumar Singh
profile.tagline: 4 deployed products — MERN, Next.js, TypeScript, Supabase, and AI integrations shipped end to end.
profile.intro: I build full-stack products that ship to production — four live applications covering an autonomous AI career OS with a 37-table PostgreSQL schema, a job tracker with a custom Cheerio web scraper for one-click import from any job board, a multi-tenant SaaS workspace with Socket.io real-time chat, and an AI chat platform with API-enforced role-based access control. Every project owns the full stack: schema design, REST API architecture, AI integrations, billing flows, and deployment.
profile.availability: Open to full-stack and SDE-1 roles — freelancing and contract work considered for the right fit.
journey: Started MCA while restructuring web practice around end-to-end product builds — React, Node.js, Express, MongoDB, and multi-platform deployment learned not through isolated tutorials but by shipping real, working applications.

Built and deployed three production-style SaaS applications across the MERN ecosystem: an AI job tracker with Cheerio web scraping and Groq integration, a multi-tenant collaboration workspace with Razorpay billing, and an AI chat SaaS with role-based access control — each deployed with separate frontend and backend services.

Currently building Valtrexa V2, an autonomous AI career OS with a 37-table PostgreSQL schema in Supabase, multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq, TanStack Start for SSR, row-level security, and N8N workflow automation — the most architecturally complex system I have designed.
focusAreas: AI integration with Groq and OpenRouter
production SaaS patterns — auth, billing, RBAC
REST API design and multi-platform deployment
TanStack and modern React application structure

timeline.title: Started MCA and rebuilt full-stack practice around real product shipping
timeline.description: Shifted from foundational web exercises into end-to-end React, Node.js, Express, and deployment workflows — using shipped applications rather than isolated tutorials as the core learning mechanism.

timeline.title: InternPe internship and first wave of deployed SaaS products
timeline.description: Completed frontend development internship while simultaneously deploying three production SaaS applications — AI job tracker, team collaboration workspace, and AI chat platform — each with auth, APIs, and real third-party integrations.

timeline.title: Expanded into PostgreSQL, TanStack, and multi-model AI architecture
timeline.description: Moved beyond MERN into PostgreSQL with Supabase, TanStack Start for SSR, multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq, and N8N workflow automation — all applied in the Valtrexa V2 build.

timeline.title: Shipping Valtrexa V2 and deepening architecture fundamentals
timeline.description: Finalizing Valtrexa V2 — a 37-table PostgreSQL career OS with autonomous AI workflows — while reinforcing DSA practice, improving API architecture depth, and targeting full-stack SDE-1 and internship roles.

projects.name: Valtrexa V2
projects.label: AI career operating system
projects.summary: An autonomous AI career OS with a 37-table PostgreSQL schema, multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq, TanStack Start SSR, Supabase RLS, and N8N workflow automation.
projects.description: Valtrexa V2 started from a real observation: job searching is fragmented across a dozen tabs — resume builder, job boards, email drafts, recruiter contacts, and calendar events with nothing connecting them. The goal was a single system that handled the entire career workflow autonomously — from resume intelligence to recruiter CRM to interview prep.
projects.challenge: Design a normalized relational schema, a multi-model AI routing layer, and an event-driven automation architecture — all while working with a stack (TanStack Start, Supabase, OpenRouter, N8N) learned entirely during the build itself.
projects.solution: Built a 37-table PostgreSQL schema in Supabase with row-level security, a TanStack Start SSR frontend with 15+ structured views, multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq with a free-tier fallback chain, PDF/DOCX document parsing, Cheerio scraping, and an event architecture wired into N8N for autonomous workflow orchestration with a Telegram command interface.
highlights: 37-table normalized PostgreSQL schema with Supabase row-level security
multi-model AI routing via OpenRouter and Groq with per-request token tracking
TanStack Start (file-based SSR) with 15+ structured views
provider abstraction for Greenhouse, Lever, and LinkedIn job import
N8N event-driven workflow automation with Telegram command interface
PDF/DOCX parsing pipeline with pdfkit, pdf-parse, and mammoth
frontendDetails: React 19 with TanStack Start — file-based SSR and structured route-based architecture
TanStack Query for server state, Zustand for client state
full Radix UI component library with react-hook-form and Zod validation
15+ views across job tracking, recruiter CRM, resume management, and interview prep
backendDetails: Supabase-managed PostgreSQL with RLS for multi-tenant data isolation
ai_generations table with per-request token and cost tracking
OpenRouter multi-model routing with free-tier fallback chain on rate limits
Cheerio and Playwright scraping pipeline with Google APIs and PDF/DOCX processing
architectureHighlights: event-driven architecture emitting workflow events into N8N for autonomous processing
provider abstraction layer supporting Greenhouse, Lever, Ashby, LinkedIn, and Wellfound
TypeScript 5 strict mode throughout with Supabase-generated type definitions
Telegram HQ command interface for real-time operational control

projects.name: JobPilot AI
projects.label: AI job application tracker
projects.summary: A full-stack AI job tracker with a Cheerio-based job page scraper, Groq-powered follow-up generation, drag-and-drop Kanban pipeline, and automated cron reminders.
projects.description: JobPilot AI was built around a specific pain: every job search spreads across a dozen browser tabs, and follow-ups never get sent because nothing tracks them. The product brings job import, tracking, AI follow-ups, reminders, and analytics into one dashboard.
projects.challenge: Build a job import system that works across every job board without site-specific configuration, and wire up AI follow-up generation, cron-driven reminders, and a Kanban workflow without making the interface feel overloaded.
projects.solution: Built a Cheerio DOM parser with a waterfall extraction strategy — itemprop, OpenGraph, CSS heuristics, page title fallback — for one-click job import from any URL. Integrated Groq (llama-3.3-70b-versatile) for follow-up email generation, DnD Kit for Kanban, node-cron for scheduled reminders, and Express 5 with native ESM modules for the backend.
highlights: Cheerio web scraper — extracts job fields from any URL using itemprop, OpenGraph, and CSS heuristics
Groq (llama-3.3-70b) for AI follow-up email generation and job summarization
drag-and-drop Kanban pipeline with DnD Kit and Redux Toolkit position persistence
node-cron automated reminders with HTML email templates via Nodemailer
Express 5 backend with native ESM modules, Swagger docs, and asyncHandler pattern
JWT + Google OAuth 2.0 with bcrypt hashing and middleware-protected routes
frontendDetails: Next.js 14 with TypeScript — structured application routing and SSR page loads
DnD Kit drag-and-drop Kanban across Applied, Interview, Offer, and Rejected columns
shadcn/ui component system with Radix primitives and Tailwind CSS
Redux Toolkit for Kanban state persistence and cross-view user context
backendDetails: Express 5 with native ESM modules, asyncHandler middleware, and Swagger/OpenAPI docs
Cheerio DOM parser with 4-stage extraction waterfall and per-field sanitization
Groq API integration with 90-second timeout, structured prompts, and HTTP error propagation
node-cron 15-minute reminder sweep with MongoDB-indexed queries and Nodemailer delivery
architectureHighlights: job scraper designed for any URL — no site-specific configuration required
Express 5 ESM backend deployed on Render with CORS-locked production config
MongoDB user-indexed queries to avoid full collection scans in cron sweeps
Cloudinary resume uploads with signed presets alongside Swagger-documented API

projects.name: DsSync Hub
projects.label: multi-tenant team collaboration SaaS
projects.summary: A multi-tenant SaaS workspace with Socket.io real-time chat, Groq AI task intelligence, Razorpay subscription billing, and a 16-model MongoDB schema.
projects.description: DsSync Hub was designed as a real SaaS product problem: team collaboration tools are either too simple or too fragmented. The goal was a focused multi-module workspace covering tasks, notes, real-time chat, and billing — each module independent but coherent within one product.
projects.challenge: Build a multi-feature SaaS product without the interface or architecture becoming fragmented — while handling multi-tenant data isolation, real-time chat, AI integration, and subscription billing in a single coherent system.
projects.solution: Architected 16 MongoDB models covering workspaces, channels, tasks, notes, and meetings. Used Socket.io for room-scoped real-time chat, integrated Groq for free-text to structured task conversion and chat thread summarization, Razorpay for subscription billing with webhook validation, and Redux Toolkit for shared application state.
highlights: 16-model MongoDB schema — workspaces, channels, tasks, notes, meetings
Socket.io real-time chat with room-scoped messages and typing indicators
Groq AI layer — free-text to structured tasks, chat thread summarization, draft rewriting
Razorpay subscription billing with webhook handling and plan enforcement middleware
per-user AI usage-limit middleware gating Groq API access
JWT + Google OAuth 2.0 with email verification and password reset via Nodemailer
frontendDetails: React and TypeScript client with scalable module-oriented component structure
Redux Toolkit for shared auth state, workspace context, and cross-view data
Tailwind-based SaaS interface across dashboard, billing, notes, chat, and task views
socket-aware components for real-time message updates and typing feedback
backendDetails: Express API with 16 Mongoose models and workspace-scoped data isolation
Socket.io WebSocket layer for room-scoped real-time chat and typing events
Groq integration with per-user usage-limit middleware for controlled AI access
Razorpay webhooks, JWT sessions, Google OAuth, email verification, and CORS-locked deployment
architectureHighlights: 16-model MongoDB schema designed for workspace-scoped, role-based data access
real-time chat via Socket.io alongside REST API for all other product operations
Groq AI gated by per-user middleware — not just UI-level checks
separate Vercel frontend and Render backend with MongoDB Atlas connection pooling

projects.name: DevFlow AI
projects.label: AI SaaS chat platform
projects.summary: A full-stack AI chat SaaS with role-based access control, per-account usage limits enforced at the API level, Groq-powered chat, Cloudinary media handling, and Razorpay billing.
projects.description: DevFlow AI was built to practice SaaS access control patterns — specifically how usage limits, billing tiers, and AI features interact without becoming messy. The goal was a clean AI chat product where free plan limits, upgrade flows, and account controls all work correctly at the API level, not just the UI.
projects.challenge: Implement role-based access control and usage limits that are actually enforced in the backend — not just hidden in the UI — while keeping the chat interface clean and the upgrade flow frictionless.
projects.solution: Built RBAC with admin/user roles and per-account daily usage limits enforced by API middleware. Integrated Groq for AI chat with chat history persistence, Razorpay for plan upgrades with payment verification, and Cloudinary for media. Deployed Next.js frontend on Netlify and Express API on Render with HTTPS CORS allowlists.
highlights: role-based access control (RBAC) with admin/user roles enforced at the API level
per-account usage limits enforced by backend middleware — not just UI gating
Groq API chat with persistent chat history across sessions
Razorpay billing with plan upgrades and payment verification
Cloudinary media handling with signed upload presets
